sections.heading: Compose Layout Basics
sections.content: Jetpack Compose uses a constraint-based layout system. Each composable receives size constraints from its parent, measures its children, and then places them on the screen. Unlike the old View system, Compose measures each UI element only once, making layouts efficient and predictable.

sections.heading: Core Layout Composables
items: Box – stacks items on top of each other

sections.heading: Arrangement & Alignment
sections.content: Column and Row support arrangement and alignment to control how children are positioned. Arrangement controls spacing along the main axis, while alignment positions items on the cross axis.

sections.heading: Modifiers
sections.content: Modifiers define a composable’s appearance, size, behavior, and interactions. They are chained together, and their order matters. Common modifiers include size, padding, background, border, and clickable.

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Compose layouts rely on simple primitives like Column, Row, and Box. Modifiers allow developers to control layout, styling, and interactions, making UI building flexible and concise.
